name: delon-form-dynamic-schema-forms
description: Create dynamic schema-based forms using @delon/form (SF component). Use this skill when building complex forms with validation, conditional rendering, async data loading, custom widgets, and multi-step workflows. Ensures forms follow JSON Schema standards, integrate with Angular reactive forms, support internationalization, and maintain consistent validation patterns across the application.
capabilities: Generate forms from JSON Schema definitions
Implement 15+ form widget types including select, autocomplete, date, and upload
Create conditional field visibility based on form state and user input
Add custom validators for complex business rules and password policies
Build multi-step wizard forms with step navigation and data persistence
Configure responsive grid layouts for mobile, tablet, and desktop screens
author: 7Spade
securitySummary: All 81 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching without context awareness. This is pure documentation containing only Markdown code examples for Angular form development. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, or malicious patterns exist. Documentation URLs (ng-alain.com, json-schema.org) are legitimate references. Backtick patterns flagged as 'shell execution' are Markdown code block delimiters. Password validation regex patterns flagged as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' are standard form validation. No additional threats were detected.
valueStatement: Creating complex forms requires extensive boilerplate code. @delon/form enables declarative form generation using JSON Schema, reducing development time and ensuring consistent validation across your Angular application.
limitations: Requires Angular 17 or later with standalone component support
Depends on @delon/form and ng-alain libraries being installed
Does not generate complete Angular application code or backend integration
Limited to client-side form validation patterns

useCases.title: Rapid Form Development
useCases.description: Accelerate form creation by defining schemas instead of writing template boilerplate manually

useCases.title: Consistent Validation
useCases.description: Share validation rules between frontend and backend using JSON Schema standards

useCases.title: Responsive Form Layouts
useCases.description: Configure grid systems and responsive breakpoints for consistent form presentation

promptTemplates.title: Basic Form Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic @delon/form schema with name, email, and age fields using JSON Schema definitions

promptTemplates.title: Async Data Loading
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to implement cascading select dropdowns with async data loading in @delon/form

promptTemplates.title: Custom Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a custom validator to confirm password fields match in an @delon/form schema

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Step Wizard
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a multi-step wizard form with Angular signals that collects user info across 3 steps

bestPractices: Define required fields in the schema to ensure proper validation feedback
Use appropriate widget types matching your data type such as string, number, boolean, or select
Implement async data loading for dynamic dropdowns to reduce initial payload size
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded string values instead of enum definitions for selectable options
Skipping form error handling which leaves users without validation feedback
Defining complex validator logic inline rather than extracting to reusable functions

faq.question: Which Angular versions are supported?
faq.answer: @delon/form requires Angular 17 or later with standalone component support enabled

faq.question: What is the maximum number of form fields?
faq.answer: No strict limit exists. Performance depends on browser capabilities and validation complexity

faq.question: Can I integrate with reactive forms?
faq.answer: Yes, SFComponent provides events for integration with Angular reactive forms and state management

faq.question: Is user input data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No, this skill only provides documentation. Form data handling depends on your application implementation

faq.question: How do I debug form validation issues?
faq.answer: Use the formError event handler to capture and log validation errors for troubleshooting

faq.question: How does this compare to Angular reactive forms?
faq.answer: @delon/form adds declarative schema-based generation while still supporting custom validators and reactive patterns
